description: Make /ci-status repo-agnostic — derive $REPO at runtime instead of hardcoding mifunedev/openharness, and add a PR-first status path via gh pr checks.

userStories.title: Parameterize the repo and add a PR-first status path in /ci-status
userStories.description: As an agent running /ci-status from a fork, I want the skill to derive its target repo at runtime and check an open PR's checks directly, so that CI status is reported for the real branch/PR instead of failing silently against the wrong upstream repo.
acceptanceCriteria: Step 1 of .claude/skills/ci-status/SKILL.md derives REPO=$(gh repo view --json nameWithOwner --jq .nameWithOwner), honors an explicit --repo owner/name override, and notes gh repo view resolves the checkout's origin repo

Step 1 includes an empty-$REPO guard: if $REPO is empty after derivation, the skill halts with a diagnostic (e.g. 'ERROR: could not derive repo — is gh authenticated? Try: gh auth login') instead of issuing a repos//... call

The skill derives PR_NUMBER explicitly via gh pr list --head "$BRANCH" --state open --repo "$REPO" --json number --jq '.[0].number' before any gh pr checks call; states that with multiple open PRs sharing the head branch the first/most-recent is used, and empty PR_NUMBER routes to the branch-runs fallback

A PR-first status path is an explicit ordered instruction step: when PR_NUMBER is non-empty the skill uses gh pr checks "$PR_NUMBER" --repo "$REPO" (and/or gh pr view --json statusCheckRollup) BEFORE any gh api repos/$REPO/actions/runs call
When PR_NUMBER is non-empty but gh pr checks reports no checks, the skill reports NO-RUN (reusing the existing NO-RUN diagnostic), not a false PASS
Every literal repos/mifunedev/openharness/ occurrence is replaced with repos/$REPO/, preserving the existing poll/result/failure-detail logic for the no-PR fallback
The NO-RUN diagnostic and CI Pipeline Steps sections carry a one-line caveat that their workflow names/steps reflect this harness's layout and may differ in other checkouts
When --repo is supplied with a value not matching owner/name format, the skill emits an error before making any gh call
grep 'gh repo view' .claude/skills/ci-status/SKILL.md returns at least one hit; grep 'gh pr checks' .claude/skills/ci-status/SKILL.md returns at least one hit
The YAML frontmatter parses without error and the numbered instruction sequence stays coherent with no dangling references
